title: [CODEQL] Missing Workflow Permissions in tests-sqlite.yml (Alert #5)
body:
## CodeQL Alert #5
**Rule:** ctions/missing-workflow-permissions (warning)  
**File:** .github/workflows/tests-sqlite.yml:12  
**URL:** https://github.com/blyartmobil/snipe-it_custom_components/security/code-scanning/5

### Description
Actions job or workflow does not limit the permissions of the GITHUB_TOKEN.

### Recommendation
Add an explicit permissions block:
`yaml
permissions:
  contents: read
`

title: [CODEQL] Insecure Randomness in pGenerator.jquery.js (Alert #6)
body:
## CodeQL Alert #6
**Rule:** js/insecure-randomness (warning)  
**File:** esources/assets/js/extensions/pGenerator.jquery.js:135  
**URL:** https://github.com/blyartmobil/snipe-it_custom_components/security/code-scanning/6

### Description
Uses Math.random() in a security context (password generation). This is cryptographically insecure and predictable.

### Recommendation
Replace with crypto.getRandomValues() or a CSPRNG-based approach for password generation.

title: [CODEQL] XSS Through DOM in snipeit.js (Alert #11)
body:
## CodeQL Alert #11
**Rule:** js/xss-through-dom (warning)  
**File:** esources/assets/js/snipeit.js:152  
**URL:** https://github.com/blyartmobil/snipe-it_custom_components/security/code-scanning/11

### Description
DOM text is reinterpreted as HTML without escaping meta-characters.

### Recommendation
Sanitize or escape HTML content before injecting into the DOM. Use 	extContent instead of innerHTML, or use a sanitization library.

title: [LOW] 7.8: No Bulk Checkin API Endpoint (Asymmetry with Bulk Checkout)
body:
## Bug ID: 7.8 (New - Round 2)
**Files:** outes/api.php, pp/Http/Controllers/Api/ComponentsController.php
**Severity:** LOW (Feature Gap)

### Issue
The API provides POST components/{component}/checkout (bulk checkout of multiple serials) but only POST components/serials/{serialId}/checkin (single checkin).

### Impact
Asymmetric workflow - checking out 10 serials is one API call, but checking them back in requires 10 separate API calls.

### Recommendation
Add a POST components/{component}/checkin bulk endpoint accepting an array of serial_ids.

title: [LOW] 7.7: ComponentPolicy Relies Entirely on Parent for Checkout/Checkin
body:
## Bug ID: 7.7 (New - Round 2)
**File:** pp/Policies/ComponentPolicy.php (Lines 1-18)
**Severity:** LOW (Information)

### Issue
The policy has no explicit checkout() or checkin() methods, relying entirely on CheckoutablePermissionsPolicy. The component serial model differs from bulk-qty checkout.

### Recommendation
Audit CheckoutablePermissionsPolicy for component serial context, or add explicit checkout/checkin overrides to ComponentPolicy.

title: [LOW] 7.6: getClone() Does Not Carry Forward Serials
body:
## Bug ID: 7.6 (New - Round 2)
**File:** pp/Http/Controllers/Components/ComponentsController.php (Lines 287-299)
**Severity:** LOW (Feature Gap)

### Issue
The clone method copies the component metadata but does not carry forward its serials to the new component. The edit form for the cloned component shows an empty serials textarea.

### Impact
Incomplete clone experience - users must manually re-enter all serial numbers.

### Fix
Populate the cloned component's serials textarea with the original serials, or explicitly note in the UI that serials must be re-entered.

title: [LOW] 7.5: totalCostSum() Decreases When Serials Are Retired
body:
## Bug ID: 7.5 (New - Round 2)
**File:** pp/Models/Component.php (Lines 404-408)
**Severity:** LOW

### Issue
	otalCostSum() calculates $this->qty * ->purchase_cost. After syncQtyFromSerials(), qty excludes retired serials. Retiring a serial reduces the reported total cost, which is semantically incorrect - the cost was already incurred.

### Impact
Financial reporting shows decreasing total cost as serials are retired, which misrepresents historical expenditure.

### Fix
Total cost should use serials()->count() (all serials ever created) or be a separately stored field, independent of the qty denormalization.

title: [LOW] 7.3: Falsy Note Handling in ComponentSerial (Elvis vs Null Coalescing)
body:
## Bug ID: 7.3 (New - Round 2)
**File:** pp/Models/ComponentSerial.php (Lines 82, 95)
**Severity:** LOW

### Issue
Uses $note ?: ->notes (Elvis operator). An empty string '' is falsy, so explicitly clearing a note silently falls back to the old value.

### Impact
Users cannot clear a serial's notes; the old note persists silently.

### Fix
Replace $note ?: ->notes with $note ?? ->notes (null coalescing) to only fall back when 
ull, respecting explicit empty strings.

title: [LOW] 4.2: Missing Select-All JavaScript on Checkout Page
body:
## Bug ID: 4.2
**File:** esources/views/components/checkout.blade.php (Line 68)
**Severity:** LOW

### Issue
The "Select All" checkbox (<input type="checkbox" id="select_all_serials">) is present in the HTML but has no corresponding JavaScript implementation to toggle the serial checkboxes.

### Fix
Add a <script> block or moar_scripts section with a click handler on #select_all_serials that toggles all .serial-checkbox inputs.

title: [MEDIUM] 7.4: listSerials Uses Global Auth Check, Not Instance Check
body:
## Bug ID: 7.4 (New - Round 2)
**File:** pp/Http/Controllers/Api/ComponentsController.php (Line 368)
**Severity:** MEDIUM (Security)

### Issue
listSerials authorizes 'view', Component::class (global view permission) but does not scope-check that the user can view *this specific* component. The web controller show() uses $this->authorize('view', ) (instance-based).

### Impact
A user with global components.view could potentially see serials of components outside their company scope in multi-company deployments.

### Fix
Change to $this->authorize('view', ).

title: [MEDIUM] 4.1: N+1 Count Queries in API Component Listing
body:
## Bug ID: 4.1
**File:** pp/Http/Transformers/ComponentsTransformer.php (Lines 73-75) + pp/Http/Controllers/Api/ComponentsController.php (Lines 64-65)
**Severity:** MEDIUM (Performance)

### Issue
Transformer supports withCount() fallback but the API index query never calls withCount(['serials', 'availableSerials', 'checkedOutSerials']). A listing of 50 components results in 150 extra database queries.

### Fix
Add eager loading with conditional counts to the index query:
`php
->withCount('serials')
->withCount(['serials as available_serials_count' => fn() => ->where('status','available')])
->withCount(['serials as checked_out_serials_count' => fn() => ->where('status','checked_out')])
`

title: [MEDIUM] 3.3: Ghost Serials in Web Update with No User Feedback
body:
## Bug ID: 3.3
**File:** pp/Http/Controllers/Components/ComponentsController.php (Lines 199-216)
**Severity:** MEDIUM

### Issue
If a user edits the serials textarea and removes a serial that is currently **checked out**, the code (correctly) skips deleting it. However, if they also added a new string (thinking they were renaming the checked-out serial), a new record is created. The user has no UI feedback that their "rename" or "delete" of a checked-out item failed.

### Impact
The old serial record remains checked out in the DB but hidden from the textarea. A new record is created as available. User has no indication of partial failure.

### Fix
Add UI warning when checked-out serials are removed from the textarea: "X serials are currently checked out and cannot be deleted." Return flash message with count of skipped serials.

title: [HIGH] 7.2: Duplicate Serials in Single API Request Bypass Validation
body:
## Bug ID: 7.2 (New - Round 2)
**File:** pp/Http/Controllers/Api/ComponentsController.php (Lines 400-428, storeSerial)
**Severity:** HIGH

### Issue
The unique:component_serials,serial,NULL,id,component_id,{->id} rule checks against the database but does NOT catch duplicates within the same request payload. Sending {serials: [{serial: 'ABC'}, {serial: 'ABC'}]} passes validation but triggers a database constraint violation on the second insert, causing a 500 error.

### Fix
Add Laravel's distinct validation rule or deduplicate $request->input('serials') before processing.

title: [HIGH] 7.1: API Checkout Event Fires Outside DB Transaction
body:
## Bug ID: 7.1 (New - Round 2)
**File:** pp/Http/Controllers/Api/ComponentsController.php (Lines 312-325)
**Severity:** HIGH

### Issue
The CheckoutableCheckedOut event is dispatched *after* the DB transaction commits. If the event listener/mailer fails, the serial records are already persisted but the action log/notification is incomplete.

### Impact
Inconsistent audit trail - serials are marked checked out but no ction_log entry exists.

### Fix
Move event(new CheckoutableCheckedOut(...)) inside the DB::transaction() closure.

title: [HIGH] 3.2: Web Checkout Event Fires Outside DB Transaction
body:
## Bug ID: 3.2
**File:** pp/Http/Controllers/Components/ComponentCheckoutController.php (Line 125)
**Severity:** HIGH

### Issue
Web controllers now wrap serial operations in DB::transaction(). However, ComponentCheckoutController fires the CheckoutableCheckedOut event *outside* the transaction.

### Impact
If the event listener fails, the serial records remain committed but the action log is missing. Partial system state - serials are marked checked out but no audit trail exists.

### Fix
Move event(...) inside the transaction closure so the entire operation is atomic.

title: [CRITICAL] 2.4: Migration Creates Ghost Serials for Components with qty=0
body:
## Bug ID: 2.4
**File:** database/migrations/2026_05_09_000000_create_component_serials_table.php (Lines 44-47)
**Severity:** CRITICAL

### Issue
The migration forces $qty = 1 if the current component quantity is less than 1.

### Impact
Components that were intentionally at 0 quantity (out of stock) will suddenly have 1 "TEMP" serial record created and marked as available. This creates "ghost" inventory.

### Fix
Skip components where $qty < 1 entirely (use if ( < 1) { continue; }), or only create serials equal to the actual non-zero quantity.

title: [CRITICAL] 2.1: Migration Data Loss - Bulk Checkouts Ignored
body:
## Bug ID: 2.1
**File:** database/migrations/2026_05_09_000000_create_component_serials_table.php (Lines 62-80)
**Severity:** CRITICAL

### Issue
The migration logic for the components_assets pivot table only updates **one** serial record to checked_out status per pivot entry. It ignores the ssigned_qty column.

### Impact
If a component was checked out to an asset with a quantity of 5, only 1 serial record will be marked as checked out. The other 4 units will appear as "available" in the new system, even though they are physically assigned to an asset.

### Fix
The inner loop must iterate by ssigned_qty (or ssigned_qty times) for each pivot record, creating/finding ssigned_qty serial records to mark as checked out.
